prompt:
Use case: stylized-concept
Asset type: project-bound RPG world atlas map, first batch pass, landscape orientation
Primary request: Warhammer Fantasy / The Old World inspired grimdark fantasy map, continent plus archipelago terrain type.
Scene/backdrop: overhead birds-eye atlas view of one dominant grim fantasy continent with a large uneven offshore archipelago.
Subject: one main old-world continent with complex coastlines, peninsulas, gulfs, bays, fjords, broken coasts, river deltas, inland seas, and many irregular island chains offshore. Islands must be unevenly distributed with varied sizes and shapes: large bleak islands, tiny islets, volcanic islands, reef rings, lagoons, narrow channels, and scattered remote atolls. Include old empire cities, river trading towns, dwarf mountain holds, blighted marshes, northern wastes, chaos-storm coast, dark corrupted forests, and fortified sea lanes.
Style/medium: dark fantasy map, old world cartography, sinister wilderness regions marked with non-text symbols, stained aged parchment, highly detailed hand-painted RPG sourcebook atlas.
Composition/framing: wide 16:9 landscape, dominant continent offset to one side and archipelago sweeping diagonally around it, no simple grid spacing, full map visible.
Lighting/mood: grimdark low fantasy, oppressive threatening sky, corruption spreading from the north, crumbling empire, dangerous seas and remote cursed islands.
Color palette: smoky parchment and cold teal ocean, charcoal ink, muted greens, rust reds, sickly marsh tones, icy northern blues, dark mountain greys.
Materials/textures: stained parchment, engraved coastlines, hand-painted terrain, soot and weathering, fine atlas detail.
Constraints: no text labels, no letters, no readable words, no legend, no compass text, no watermark, no UI, no characters or portraits. Islands should feel natural and irregular, not evenly spaced.
